channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic-led states sue to block Trump administration layoffs at the Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of Democratic-led states is challenging the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs across the Education Department, saying it amounts to an illegal dismantling of an agency created by Congress.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione’s journal not ‘manifesto’ about healthcare industry grievances, attorney argues
item.description: Luigi Mangione's Pennsylvania defense attorney argues that authorities' use of the word 'manifesto' to describe UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect's 'personal writings' is 'incorrect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijack and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif was in the restive southwestern Balochistan province on Thursday to meet survivors of a train attack and the commandos who rescued over 300 passengers from insurgents who killed 21 civilian and four troops.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Proof of citizenship may be required to vote under new Texas proposal
item.description: Senate Bill 16 in Texas proposes requiring proof of U.S. citizenship for voter registration, affecting new voters and those currently registered.
item.dc:creator: Re'Chelle Turner, Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-Texas megachurch pastor indicted in Oklahoma on child sex abuse charges
item.description: A former pastor of a Texas megachurch who resigned after a woman accused him of sexually abusing her in the 1980s has been indicted in Oklahoma on child sexual abuse charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: The fate of the IRS Direct File program remains unclear as Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency cleave their way through the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Employee confidence at 'record low': Glassdoor survey
item.description: "Rising economic uncertainty is tamping down employee sentiment and also stretching employees thin," a Glassdoor economist said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, ALEXA ST. JOHN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Marine mammals in California getting sick from algae: Care center
item.description: "The number of current MMCC patients likely impacted by the toxic algal bloom has more than tripled," Marine Mammal Care said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's CEO wants to speed up the process it takes to introduce new burgers
item.description: McDonald’s CEO Chris Kempczinski said he intends to introduce new burgers and improve the chain’s signature burgers at a faster rate.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Child illegally taken by her mother from US to Mexico in 1999 is found and reconnects with dad
item.description: Carlos Reyes-Couvertier spent the past quarter century trying to find his missing daughter. There were multiple trips to Mexico, where police believed she was living after having been abducted by her mother.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Jay-Z accuser admits claim fabricated in audio recording: ABC
item.description: The woman who sued Jay-Z alleging he raped her when she was 13 years old has been heard on tape admitting that the assault did not occur and that her lawyer pushed her to sue, according to a report by ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: Those who are over the age of 14 will also need to apply for fingerprinting with authorities, according to the mandate uploaded Wednesday to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 20 charged with murder after 3 die inside Nevada prison
item.description: Charges against the 20 defendants include first-degree murder and attempted murder with gang enhancements, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors say Kohberger's defense will argue evidence was planted in Idaho student murders case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors say Bryan Kohberger’s defense team will argue at his murder trial that someone else could have planted a knife sheath with Kohberger’s DNA at the home where four University of Idaho students were killed in 2022.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeanne Shaheen to retire, setting up battle for New Hampshire Senate seat
item.description: “It was a difficult decision, made more difficult by the current environment in the country — by President Trump and what he’s doing right now,” said Shaheen, who is 78.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, KRON4
